title: pyrus rhombiformis
intro: Pyrus rhombiformis, also known as the Japanese rowan or Japanese mountain-ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Pyrus rhombiformis include reducing fever, alleviating gastrointestinal issues, and acting as a natural diuretic, as well as providing antioxidant protection against free radicals. Pyrus rhombiformis can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
